What triggers it: customer_id IS NULL (after coalescing CustomerID, Customer_ID, cust_id)
Why that consequence: A customer record with no identifier cannot be linked to any policy or claim. Compliance needs to review the source file and region

Rule: region must be a valid value
What triggers it: region NOT IN ('East', 'West', 'Central', 'South', 'North') after standardizing W/C/E/S
Why that consequence: Invalid region breaks every regional report and aggregation. Compliance team must trace back to the source system

What triggers it: Duplicate rows on customer_id + region + state + city
Why that consequence: Duplicates inflate policyholder counts which is the exact regulatory problem PrimeInsurance is under scrutiny for

What triggers it: education NOT IN ('primary', 'secondary', 'tertiary', NULL) after fixing terto typo and NA strings
Why that consequence: Missing or unusual education is not critical for business operations. Log it but let the record through

What triggers it: marital NOT IN ('single', 'married', 'divorced', NULL) after fixing swapped columns in customers_6
Why that consequence: Marital status is informational. Log the anomaly but don't block the record
A claim with no ID cannot be tracked or processed. Must be investigated
A claim with no policy reference is unlinkable. Cannot determine coverage or customer

What triggers it: CAST(injury AS DOUBLE) < 0 OR CAST(property AS DOUBLE) < 0 OR CAST(vehicle AS DOUBLE) < 0 (after converting NULL strings to actual null)
Why that consequence: Negative claim amounts indicate data corruption. These would distort total claim value calculations

Rule: Claim_Rejected must be Y or N
Why that consequence: Rejection status drives the rejection rate metric which is a primary business KPI. Invalid values make the metric unreliable

Rule: string NULL and ? converted to actual null
What triggers it: Claim_Processed_On = 'NULL' OR property_damage = '?' OR collision_type = '?' OR police_report_available = '?'
Why that consequence: These are data entry placeholders not real values. We convert them to proper nulls and log how many were found per column

Rule: numeric fields must be castable to number
What triggers it: injury, property, vehicle cannot be cast to double
Why that consequence: If a claim amount is not a valid number after removing NULL strings it is corrupt data
Policy number is the primary key and the link between customers and claims. Without it the record is useless
A policy with no customer cannot be attributed. Breaks the customer-policy-claim join chain
Zero or negative premium is either a data error or a cancelled policy that should not appear in active records
Found 1 record with -1000000. Negative coverage limits are invalid. Compliance must review

Rule: policy_csl must be a known tier
Why that consequence: Unknown coverage tier may indicate a new product. Log it but don't block
3132 entirely empty rows (62.9% of sales data) have null sales_id. These are blank padding rows from the source files, not real listings
A listing with zero or negative price is invalid. Cannot be used for revenue analysis

Rule: ad_placed_on must be a valid date
What triggers it: ad_placed_on cannot be parsed as timestamp from dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm format
Why that consequence: If we cannot determine when a car was listed we cannot calculate days_listed or aging metrics
car_id is the primary key and the link between policy/sales and the vehicle. Without it the record is orphaned
Negative kilometers driven is physically impossible and indicates data corruption
